title: Watch: Donald Trump ducks for cover as security rushes to evacuate him
content: Panic erupted at the White House Correspondents' Dinner as gunshots were heard, forcing President Trump and Vice President Vance to be swiftly evacuated. Secret Service agents secured the venue, apprehending a suspect. Despite the scare, Trump praised security and declared "the show must go on." The incident caused widespread confusion and a massive security response.

title: Engine fire on SWISS Delhi-Zurich flight during take off roll; aircraft evacuated on runway
content: Six passengers were hospitalized after a SWISS flight from Delhi to Zurich was evacuated on the runway due to an engine fire during takeoff. The Airbus A330's engine number one failed at 104 knots, prompting the crew to abort the takeoff and evacuate all 232 passengers and crew using emergency slides.

title: 'Dear cricket, give me one more chance': Karun Nair's old post resurfaces
content: Karun Nair endured a tough night as Delhi Capitals lost to Punjab Kings, who chased a record 265. Nair dropped Shreyas Iyer twice, allowing him to score 71 and seal the win. The moment went viral, with fans resurfacing Nair’s old “Dear cricket” post. Visibly shaken, the 34-year-old’s struggles highlighted both the cost of missed chances and his ongoing journey of resilience.

title: ‘Taken offsite’: Who was the shooter at Trump’s White House dinner? First image emerges
content: President Donald Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots were fired, with the suspect apprehended by the Secret Service. An individual reportedly attempted to enter with a gun but was stopped before reaching the ballroom. Trump praised law enforcement and urged the event to continue.

title: Blue Origin’s rockets whose photos Bezos shared & Musk congratulated 'failed' minutes later
content: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket achieved a successful booster landing but failed to deliver its satellite payload to the correct orbit. This marks the program's first major mission failure. The FAA has grounded the rocket pending an investigation into the upper stage engine issue, impacting future flights.

title: 'Quite an evening': Trump wanted 'the show to go on' after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
content: Shots were fired near the White House Correspondents' Dinner venue, prompting President Trump's temporary evacuation as a security precaution. The shooter was apprehended by law enforcement, who acted swiftly and bravely. Trump confirmed the incident and indicated plans to reschedule the event, emphasizing the safety of all attendees.

title: Watch: Sooryavanshi now has Aussie fan; Cummins's ‘new favourite player’
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dazzled with a 37-ball 103, powering Rajasthan Royals to 228/6, earning praise from Pat Cummins, who called him his “new favourite player.” Supported by Dhruv Jurel’s 51, RR set a strong total. However, Sunrisers Hyderabad chased it down, led by Ishan Kishan’s 74 and Abhishek Sharma’s 57, sealing a thrilling win.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints Rs 2.95 cr amid ‘Michael' and ‘Bhooth Bangla’ buzz
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its strong box office run, nearing Rs 1,130 crore in India and Rs 1,780 crore worldwide after 38 days. Despite competition, the film saw significant growth on its 6th Saturday, earning Rs 2.95 crore domestically.  Meanwhile, 'Bhooth Bangla' and 'Michael' also performed on Saturday.

title: Shooter fires 5 to 8 shots at White House dinner, Trump evacuated - what we know so far
content: A major security incident disrupted the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, forcing the evacuation of President Trump and other top officials. Witnesses reported hearing five to eight gunshots, and a shooter was confirmed by law enforcement, though details remain unclear. No injuries were immediately reported among the hundreds of guests.

title: Starting January 1, Deloitte cuts parental leave, PTO, pension, IVF funding in US
content: Deloitte plans to reduce paid family leave from 16 to eight weeks and cut annual paid time off by 5-10 days for a segment of its US workforce starting January 1, 2027. These changes, affecting internal roles under the 'Center' talent model, aim to tailor benefits to market alignment. New pension plan contributions and adoption/surrogacy reimbursements will also cease.

title: Youngest. Fastest. Unstoppable! Sooryavanshi scripts history yet again
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has shattered T20 cricket records, becoming the youngest and fastest to 1,000 runs in just 473 balls. His recent 36-ball century, featuring 12 sixes, marks his second IPL hundred under 40 balls, placing him in an elite group. Sooryavanshi also holds the record for the quickest 50 IPL sixes.

title: Delhi sizzles as mercury hits 42.8°C, hottest April day in 4 years
content: Delhi experienced its hottest April day in four years on Saturday, with temperatures reaching 42.8 degrees Celsius. Heatwave conditions are expected to persist through Sunday and Monday, though light rain may bring some relief from Tuesday onwards. The city's air quality remains in the 'poor' category.

title: KPMG to lay off 10% of US audit team after failed retirement efforts
content: KPMG is set to cut approximately 10% of its US audit partners after voluntary retirement schemes failed to yield desired results. The accounting giant, one of the Big Four, cited a need to align its partnership size with current business demands.

title: Ronaldo’s 3 final career goals before retirement – Messi already ahead
content: Cristiano Ronaldo is entering the final phase of his career with three clear goals still driving him forward: a first World Cup title in 2026, the pursuit of 1,000 career goals, and the rare chance of playing alongside his son at Al Nassr. Even at 41, he remains central for club and country, shaping both legacy and milestones.

title: Patrick Mahomes & Brittany Mahomes hit $325m valuation amid recovery
content: Patrick Mahomes, the Kansas City Chiefs’ quarterback, is focused on his recovery from his brutal injuries as his wife, Brittany Mahomes stands by his side through all the difficult times. While both Patrick Mahomes and his wife, Brittany Mahomes, have preferred to be private about the star player’s recovery, the high profile couple recently received an update worth a whopping $325 million.

title: Titans uncover affair? Mike Vrabel exit linked to Russini drama
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, has taken some time off from work to be with his family as his alleged affair rumors with Dianna Russini continue to get worse every day. Though the New England Patriots have not yet hinted at firing Mike Vrabel ahead of the new season, it seems his time with the Tennessee Titans is being viewed differently.

title: IRS officer daughter rape-murder: Accused made to retrace footsteps to find out how he entered
content: A former domestic worker was taken to the home of a senior bureaucrat in Delhi to recreate the crime scene where he allegedly raped and killed the officer's daughter. The accused described his actions, including accessing an electronic safe, and his plan to flee to Rajasthan with stolen valuables.

title: Karun Nair left devastated, on the verge of tears as PBKS script record chase
content: Karun Nair's dropped catches proved costly as Punjab Kings achieved the IPL's highest successful chase, overcoming Delhi Capitals' 265. Shreyas Iyer, granted two lifelines by Nair, capitalized with a match-winning 71. Earlier, Prabhsimran Singh and Priyansh Arya's explosive opening stand set the stage for the record-breaking victory.

title: Vabihav Sooryavanshi injury scare: 15-year-old leaves the field in pain
content: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, aged 15, achieved a record-breaking 103 off 37 balls in IPL 2026, becoming the youngest to 1,000 T20 runs. He later suffered an injury scare, appearing to be battling severe cramps after his explosive innings. Despite his heroics, Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a thrilling five-wicket victory.

title: Power demand defies forecasts, officials say consumption likely to surge further
content: India's electricity demand hit a record 252 GW on April 24. Soaring temperatures across the nation drove this surge. Air conditioner usage increased significantly in homes and businesses. This peak demand surpassed previous records. The power ministry anticipates further increases as heatwaves continue. Officials expect demand to rise even higher in the coming days.

title: Imphal erupts as protesters clash with police over killing of kids
content: A massive turnout rocked Imphal as protesters clashed with police, thwarted in their attempt to march to the Chief Minister's abode. The Coordinating Committee on Manipur Integrity (Cocomi) seized the moment to issue a memorandum, pressing for clarity on issues of a 'proxy war,' the escalation of narco-terrorism, accountability for recent murders, and the prompt adoption of NRC measures.

title: 'Too much time wasted': Trump scraps US envoys' visit to Pak, says Iran can 'call' for talks
content: US President Donald Trump canceled a planned trip by his envoys to Pakistan to meet Iranian leaders, citing wasted travel time and confusion within Iran's leadership. He asserted the US holds the upper hand and urged Iran to initiate talks. This decision has diminished Pakistan's role in facilitating US-Iran discussions.

title: NIT Silchar bans prayer, religious gathering in open
content: To ensure a harmonious living environment, NIT Silchar has officially prohibited religious gatherings in common hostel spaces. This measure follows accusations from the Bajrang Dal about potential 'jihadi activities' and allegations of favoritism towards Hindu students. Though private prayer sessions are still allowed, those who disregard this directive may face disciplinary consequences.

title: No ballot papers, no ink, no boxes: Inside Gaza's first election in two decades
content: Gaza witnessed a historic moment as Palestinians engaged in their first local elections in over two decades, marking a tentative step toward reconciling the fragmented political landscape between Gaza and the West Bank. Though hampered by Israeli-imposed restrictions, officials creatively utilised whatever materials they could muster to ensure the elections went ahead.

title: Head in hands: Vipraj Nigam left stunned as Karun Nair drops Iyer twice - Watch
content: PBKS captain Shreyas Iyer got two major lifelines during the chase and made Delhi pay with a 71-run knock off 36 balls.

title: 'Humiliating defeat': Iran reminds US of 46-yr-old failure - what was Op Eagle Claw?
content: Iran's judiciary chief warned the US of repeating "Isfahan and Tabas" incidents if tensions escalate in the Persian Gulf. This warning coincides with the 46th anniversary of the failed US Operation Eagle Claw in 1980, a mission to rescue American hostages in Tehran that ended in disaster and casualties.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office day 38: Film slowly inches towards Rs 1800 cr worldwide
content: In a stunning display of cinematic prowess, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' has captivated audiences, amassing nearly Rs 1800 crore globally. Surpassing the Rs 1700 crore mark, it now stands as the third highest-grossing Indian film. By its sixth week, it continues to fend off stiff competition, raking in remarkable box office figures both at home and abroad. Read on to know more in detail.

title: 'Torn pants to Rs 100 cr house': Swati Maliwal's 'traitor' jibe at Kejriwal after AAP exit
content: Former aide to Arvind Kejriwal and now BJP leader Swati Maliwal has accused the ex-Delhi CM of hypocrisy, claiming he began his political journey in torn pants with a two-rupee pen and a dilapidated car, but after coming to power built a house worth Rs 100 crore.She also criticised the AAP's governance in Punjab. The remarks follow a significant party exodus.

title: Pinned On Ground: Trump Releases Photo Of White House Press Dinner Shooter
content: The suspected shooter has been identified as 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen of Torrance, California, according to reports.

title: Trump Event Shooting Live Updates: Trump Says Press Dinner Shooter Was "Probably Alone"
content: Shooting at Trump's event live updates: Donald Trump was rushed out of the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after suspected gunshots were heard at the event.

title: Video: Trump Ducks For Cover, Then Evacuated As Shots Heard At Press Dinner
content: President Donald Trump was reported uninjured and other top leaders of the United States were evacuated from an annual dinner.

title: All Not Well In Uddhav Sena? MPs' 'Courtesy' Meetings Spark Exodus Buzz
content: Sources familiar with recent meetings among the MPs in Delhi point to a deeper issue -- an alleged communication gap between the party's leadership and its parliamentary wing.

title: Gunmen Attack Several Locations Across Mali's Capital
content: Mali's army said in a statement that "unidentified armed terrorist groups targeted certain locations and barracks" in Bamako and that soldiers were "engaged in eliminating the attackers."

title: China Hints Its 4th Aircraft Carrier Will Be Nuclear-Powered
content: The video film 'Into The Deep' was released on Wednesday to mark the 77th anniversary of the founding of the People's Liberation Army Navy showcased Chinese navy's transition from maritime ambitions and coastal defences.

title: Will It, Won't It? Trump's Big Move Casts Shadow Over US-Iran Peace Talks
content: According to Fox News, Trump said that he told the envoys not to get on an "18-hour flight" while they were getting ready to leave. He said that he does not want his officials to "sit around talking about nothing".

title: Survival, Legal Fight, Punjab Battle: What's Next for AAP After MP Exodus
content: The AAP has said it will seek the disqualification of the seven MPs and take the legal route if there is a delay.

title: "Blindness": Court Asks Tamil Nadu To Roll Out Transgender Rehabilitation Plan
content: The court reiterated that transgender persons are protected under Articles 14, 15, 16 and 21, which guarantee equality and the right to live with dignity.

title: 4 Killed In Israeli Airstrikes On Lebanon Amid Fragile Ceasefire
content: Israel and Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah have continued to clash in southern Lebanon despite reduced hostilities due to the ceasefire.

title: Delhi Records Hottest Day Of Year At 42.8 Degrees, Yellow Alert Issued
content: Across Delhi, the impact is already being felt through blistering afternoons and unusually warm nights, with temperatures well above normal.

title: 'Iran Can Call Us Anytime': Trump After Cancelling Top Officials' Pak Trip
content: Trump says he has cancelled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner's trip to Pak for Iran talks: News agency AFP citing Fox News

title: 15-Year-Old Boy Killed After Speeding Tempo Hits Bike In Delhi: Cops
content: A 15-year-old boy was killed on Saturday evening after an overspeeding tempo hit his motorcycle from behind in Shalimar Bagh here, police said.

title: Raghav Chadha Loses 1.1 Million Instagram Followers After AAP Rebellion
content: As of now, his follower count has declined to 13.1 million, indicating that nearly 1.1 million users have unfollowed him within just 24 hours.

title: Fabulous Lives Of Cartel Wives: Women Masterminds Of Latin Drug Businesses
content: The recent killing of the world's most-wanted traffickers, Nemesio "El Mencho" Oseguera Cervantes, in a daring operation by the Mexican military has revealed that sometimes it is the cartel women that you need to watch out for.

title: U.S. sanctions waiver on Chabahar port ends on April 26, could signal end of 23-year-old connectivity project
content: Officials say they are discussing a possible transfer of IPGL-subsidiary’s stake to local Iranian company temporarily to avoid sanctions

title: UDF will probe “corruption” in the cancellation of DBFOO power supply contracts if it comes to power: V.D. Satheesan
content: Leader of the Opposition blames the LDF govt. for the decision to cancel these contracts, says the move has led to unscheduled loadsheddings and power restrictions across the State this summer

title: Kavitha’s ‘TRS’ may face ECI scrutiny over acronym  similarity
content: In Kavitha’s party’s case, the proposed name “Telangana Rashtra Sena” shares the first two words with the original TRS and carries the identical abbreviation, which the ECI may object to

title: India advises nationals in Mali to stay indoors, exercise caution due to security developments
content: The Indian embassy added that it was closely monitoring the evolving situation in coordination with Malian authorities and will issue further updates as required

title: Experts discuss preventive care and data-led approach to public health challenges
content: The webinar discussed challenges from infectious diseases to NCDs, climate-linked risks, and the growing role of AI and policy in building resilient healthcare systems

title: 'Lawrence of Punjab' docu-series: Congress leader Randhawa gets threat message
content: In the threat message, the names of other persons including Punjab Congress chief Amrinder Singh Raja Warring, SAD leader Bikram Singh Majithia, DGP Gaurav Yadav, Balkaur Singh, father of slain singer Sidhu Moosewala were also mentioned

title: Ashok Kharat case: Oshnology and other deceptions of a godman
content: Ashokkumar Kharat, who police say is a school dropout, allegedly manipulated women and men to build financial assets and political clout over two decades in Nashik. When the Maharashtra police arrested him on charges of rape and fraud, he had come to be known as Captain Kharat, claiming to have worked in the merchant navy in the past. Vinaya Deshpande Pandit speaks to victims, who tell tales of sexual exploitation, financial fraud, and so-called occult practices

title: 'Women will respond': Congress slams govt over 'delay' in 33% quota; says implement it at once
content: Congress Gurugram District President Vardhan Yadav said the issue of women's reservation is not merely political, but rather related to social justice and equality

title: Madras University invites applications from students for free education scheme
content: Applicants from economically backward strata, orphans, children of widows/widowers, first graduates of family, and transgender persons would be given preference for selection

title: Sculpture by Spanish artist Jaume Plensa unveiled at Kempegowda International Airport in Bengaluru
content: Installed in painted stainless steel and measuring 500 x 319 x 375 cm, the sculpture reflects the city’s diversity, creativity, and global outlook, said a statement from the Foundation.

title: T.N. Assembly elections: Over 50 lakh more voters exercise their franchise this time compared to 2024 Lok Sabha polls
content: In the State, people’s interest in polling process has always been higher for Assembly elections when compared to Lok Sabha polls, show data from elections held over the past 20 years; parties carry out a more intensive mobilisation for State polls

title: SFI seeks action against ‘harassment’ of medical PG students
content: All 12 PG doctors in the department have lodged a complaint with the Principal, alleging humiliation by two faculty members

title: Officials draw flak over alleged administrative lapses in Kozhikode Corporation
content: Councillors across ruling and Opposition benches demand action against officials over recurring lapses. Mayor says the Corporation will not shield those found guilty and promises strict action.

title: The killing factories of Sivakasi
content: Deaths due to firecracker unit blasts in the Sivakasi region have become a periodic occurrence, especially during summer. The recent incident, in which 25 workers died in a series of blasts, again spotlights the gross violations in the operation of firecracker factories and the inadequate monitoring of these units

title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah pitches Kashmir as ideal shooting destination to South Indian filmmakers
content: The Chief Minister highlighted J&K’s unparalleled natural beauty, diverse landscapes, and rich cultural heritage, ranging from serene valleys and snow-clad mountains to vibrant traditions and local craftsmanship

title: Vehicles stranded after avalanche hits Srinagar-Kargil highway
content: Snow sheaths started sliding into a major avalanche in the afternoon at Shaitan Nallah, which connects Sonamarg in the Kashmir Valley with Drass in Ladakh, officials say

title: Artists get creative at ‘Articulate Gunturian’
content: Their artworks showcase varied themes such as cultural heritage, women’s safety, festivals, social stigma, nature and rural life

title: Residents stage protest, seek house sites in Mangalagiri
content: Protesters demand distribution of house sites, construction support, and issuance of pattas to families living on assigned land

title: Tagged Olive Ridley turtles from T.N. reach Sri Lanka Dome, reveal migration patterns
content: Considered one of the most productive regions in the Indian Ocean, the Sri Lanka Dome phenomenon typically begins in May, peaks in July, and declines by September

title: Four booked for child marriage in Kasaragod
content: The police said the case was registered after the Child Marriage Prohibition Officer received a complaint that a minor girl from Valiyaparamba had been married off

title: Will AAP MPs face disqualification after joining BJP?
content: Is there a law against defection? What are the exceptions to it and how has the Supreme Court interpreted them? What are experts’ view on seven of AAP’s 10 Rajya Sabha members joining the BJP last week?

title: Calls to do away with elephants, fireworks in temple festivals get louder in Kerala
content: In the aftermath of the recent fireworks tragedy at Mundathikode in Thrissur, social media campaigns are gathering strength seeking to move away from the use of high-intensity gunpowder displays as well as elephants during festivities

title: War of words erupts over girl’s death in Ghazipur, U.P.; Rahul Gandhi demands immediate justice
content: Congress General Secretary and Lok Sabha MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra alleged that atrocities against women in U.P. reached its peak under the BJP rule

title: ECI suspends police officials in Bengal; Trinamool alleges strongroom breach
content: The poll body has ordered the suspension of five police officials at Diamond Harbour over ‘serious misconduct’ and ‘failure to maintain neutrality’ during Assembly polls

title: Dinesh Trivedi | A man for all seasons
content: A politician with close ties to West Bengal, the BJP leader heads to Dhaka as India’s envoy, with a mandate to reset ties with the neighbour

title: NHAI demolishes top portion of stormwater drain to create more road space in Vellore
content: A new drain will be built corresponding to the existing road level to allow more vehicles, especially buses, to navigate easily on the carriageway

title: Seven e-bikes catch fire near Ranipet town
content: The police said that a mechanic was checking the condition of batteries at the shop when the explosion happened

title: March to Manipur CM’s house foiled; 22 injured
content: They took out the march demanding action against those behind the April 7 bomb attack that killed two children

title: Concern over air quality dip in Mysuru
content: Factors contributing to poor air quality include smoking, burning of waste materials and garbage and vehicular emissions among others

title: Restoration work at Hindustan Petroleum’s Rajasthan refinery expected to complete in 3-4 weeks
content: HPCL also informed that the trial production of liquified petroleum products, motor spirit, high-speed diesel and naphtha is expected to start within May this year

title: Mapping a billion lives: How GenomeIndia Project will transform medicine, healthcare
content: A writer at Times of India, I cover the stories that live in the gaps — between a screen and real life, between what we're sold and what we actually feel. I try bring curiosity and candour to everything I write, whether I'm digging into global affairs, modern relationships, or the quieter anxieties of everyday life. Read More

title: Sebi to simplify norms to boost business ease
content:
Mumbai: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey addresses the 38th SEBI Foundation Day, at the Jio World Convention Centre, in Mumbai, Maharashtra. (PTI Photo/Kunal Patil)

MUMBAI: Markets regulator Sebi will accelerate its drive to simplify and rationalise regulations to improve ease of doing business for capital market participants, its chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey said Saturday. The regulator will collaborate to drive innovation for market development so that capital formation supports faster economic growth, he said, adding Sebi will continue investing in tech-led supervision. At the 38th Foundation Day event of Sebi, Pandey said that despite a volatile global environment, India's market resilience reflects years of institution-building, sound regulation, and strong frameworks that have enabled sustained capital formation and market activity.

End of Article

title: Ladakh lifts bar on lower-qualified govt staff from applying for graduate-level posts
content: ladakh lieutenant governor lifted regulations that barred govt employees from applying for graduate-level posts . a candidate selected for a lower-qualification job was required to submit an affidavit declaring that they were only a Class 10 or Class 12 pass . the embargo was “unjustified” and “restrictive,” but imposing limits on maximum qualifications barred candidates from future employment .

title: Relief for Farooq as Srinagar court stays proceedings against him in JKCA scam case
content: a sessions court stayed a magisterial court’s order allowing framing of charges against national conference president Dr Farooq Abdullah in the jammu and Kashmir cricket association scam case . the additional sessions judge ordered the operation of a chief judicial magistrate’s March 2 directive, and the subsequent proceedings initiated by the trial magistrate on March 30 to be kept in abeyance till the next date of hearing .

title: J&K parties back Engineer Rashid over bail to visit ailing father
content: a NIA court rejected the jailed baramulla MP’s bail plea to meet his critically ill father . his son said his grandfather was on ventilator support in the ICU of Srinagar’s SMHS Hospital . a man who has spent years in Tihar jail asked for a few moments with his father in his final hours .

title: AAP to write to RS Chairman demanding disqualification of MPs who crossed over to BJP
content: BJP president Nitin Nabin welcomes Raghav Chadha, Sandeep Pathak, Ashok Mittal, others to BJP NEW DELHI . despite the breakaway faction of AAP MPs claiming they have fortified themselves legally with two-third of the party’s members in the Upper House merging with BJP . the BJP announced that they will be writing to the Rajya Sabha Chairman C P Radhakrishnan to seek the disqualification of seven of its members

title: One nation, one KYC need of the hour: Nirmala Sitharaman
content: the govt has been working on a unified know your client (KYC) system . the system is yet to be fully operational, causing inconvenience . other regulators are seen to be lagging . "we need a seamless, secure, and portable KYC experience across the financial sector," she says .

title: Orphaned twice: SIR strips Kolkata's Kerala-born man of vote, identity
content: benji, 50, lost his mother at birth and his father soon after . he was taken in by a fast-food stall in beleghata . benji has lived with the octogenarian family since 1999 .

title: In biggest revamp, 4 new members join Niti Aayog under Ashok Lahiri
content: the govt has reconstituted Niti Aayog, appointing Ashok Lahiri as the new vice-chairman and four new members in the biggest overhaul of the thinktank since it was set up in 2015 . the change began last month wiping up a jaded look with very few fresh ideas emerging from the building .

title: Assembly polls: West Bengal turnout hits 93.2%, Tamil Nadu settles at 85.1%
content:
Representational image

KOLKAT/CHENNAI: Bengal recorded a historic 93.2% turnout in the first phase of assembly elections, while Tamil Nadu's final provisional figure settled at 85.1% after a marginal revision, EC data showed on Saturday. More than 3.36 crore voters cast ballots across 16 Bengal districts on April 23, out of a 3.6 crore electorate. Cooch Behar led with 96.2%, followed by South Dinajpur at 95.4%. Malda (94.8%), Jalpaiguri (94.7%), Birbhum (94.5%) and North Dinajpur (94.2%) also crossed 94%. Only Kalimpong (83%) and Darjeeling (88.9%) remained below 90%. Tamil Nadu's turnout was revised down by .05 percentage points, accounting for 28,672 voters and pegging total votes at 4.87 crore. Figures remain provisional. EC ruled out any re-poll after scrutiny of 75,064 polling stations. Karur district topped at 92.6%, while Karur constituency led with 93.4%. Veerapandi in Salem followed at 93.4%.

End of Article

title: Election Commission transfers 4 cops in 3 Kolkata police stations
content: four inspectors across three police stations in the city have been transferred to the special branch . the EC also announced its decision to rope in 11 more police observers for phase II polls on April 29 when polling will be held for 142 seats . a new reshuffle involves inspectors from the south division .

title: 'Stay indoors': IMD's dos & don'ts to beat the heat
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy.
